forecasts.reasoning: Kadyrov frequently floats resignation to gauge loyalty and elicit support from Putin. He made a similar public request in May 2025, but met with Putin on May 7, where the resignation was reportedly rejected. Kadyrov also walked back his comments shortly after. In addition, his son Adam is just 17, and the Chechen Constitution requires the Republic's Head to be at least 30, precluding a rushed succession. Given the June 1 deadline is less than three weeks away, Kadyrov ceasing to be head is highly improbable and would only occur via sudden death or an unprecedented black-swan political purge.

The NFL owners will meet May 20-21, 2025, providing a clear window for this to happen before the June 1 deadline. While an initial proposal by the Packers in April fell short with only about 16 teams supporting it (24 needed to pass), the issue was specifically tabled to rework the language. Recent reporting from early May (including CBS Sports and the Washington Post) indicates that a revised, expanded proposal to ban pushing/pulling the ballcarrier has broad momentum, with the league office reportedly supporting the change. Given the specific timing of the May meeting, the concerted effort to revise the proposal, and the influential weight of the league office, the ban seems more likely than not to pass, though crossing the 24-vote threshold remains a notable hurdle.
